name: Logistics & Supply-Chain Ontology (LSC-ONT)
description: Models end-to-end logistics supply chains — the journey, nodes, shipping legs, compliance gates, risk assessments, bottlenecks, incidents, escalation paths, and net impact. Designed for international trade corridors (AU meat export → UK import as reference pattern) but generalisable to any regulated cold-chain or freight corridor.

oaa:joinPatterns.name: End-to-End Journey Traversal
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Walk the full supply chain from origin to destination through ordered legs and nodes
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: lsc:SupplyChain → hasLeg → lsc:ShipmentLeg (ordered by legSequence) → connectsFrom/connectsTo → lsc:ChainNode
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Visualise the complete journey map with all waypoints

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Bottleneck Heat Map
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Overlay risk-scored bottlenecks on chain nodes to identify tight spots
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Identify highest-risk nodes and their cumulative risk exposure

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Incident Escalation Chain
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Trace an incident through its escalation levels to resolution
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map escalation pathways and identify time-to-resolution patterns

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Compliance Gate Document Flow
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Map which documents are required at each compliance gate and who issues them
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Pre-flight document readiness checks before shipment departure

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Net Impact Cascade
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Calculate cascading net impact from a bottleneck through affected shipments to financial outcome
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: lsc:Bottleneck → hasImpactProfile → lsc:ImpactAssessment; lsc:Incident → hasImpact → lsc:ImpactAssessment
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Quantify total exposure and net financial/time/reputational impact of a disruption

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Cold Chain Integrity Trace
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Verify continuous cold chain through temperature events at each node and leg
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Audit temperature compliance across the full journey

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Logistics-to-Governance Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Connect logistics compliance gates to GRC framework requirements
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Ensure logistics operations satisfy governance and regulatory obligations

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Party Context & Maturity Roll-Up
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Roll up logistics party profiles to Foundation organisation context including industry classification, maturity dimensions, and market segments
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: lsc:Party → partyMapsToOrg → org:Organization; lsc:Party → partyHasOrgContext → org-ctx:OrganizationContext → hasMaturity → org-ctx:OrganizationMaturity
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Assess third-party reliability by correlating logistics performance with organisational maturity and industry context

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Corridor Market & Jurisdiction Roll-Up
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Roll up supply chain corridor to Foundation market context and node-level jurisdictions for geographic regulatory mapping
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: lsc:SupplyChain → operatesInMarket → org-ctx:MarketContext → hasSegment → org-ctx:MarketSegment; lsc:ChainNode → nodeInJurisdiction → org-ctx:MarketSegment
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Map corridor-level market scope and per-node regulatory jurisdictions to Foundation geography for cross-corridor comparison and compliance aggregation

entities.name: SupplyChain
entities.description: An end-to-end logistics corridor connecting origin to destination. Defines the trade route, commodity type, regulatory regime, and overall chain characteristics. The top-level container for the journey graph.

oaa:properties.name: chainId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for this supply chain

oaa:properties.name: commodity
oaa:properties.description: Product being shipped (e.g. chilled beef, frozen lamb)

oaa:properties.name: tradeRoute
oaa:properties.description: Named corridor e.g. AU-UK Meat Corridor

oaa:properties.name: regulatoryRegime
oaa:properties.description: Applicable trade agreement/framework e.g. AU-UK FTA

oaa:properties.name: chainRiskScore
oaa:properties.description: Aggregate risk score 1.0-10.0 across all nodes

entities.name: ChainNode
entities.description: A waypoint, facility, or checkpoint in the supply chain. Represents physical locations where cargo dwells, is processed, inspected, or transferred. Each node has a risk profile and may have compliance gates and bottlenecks attached.

oaa:properties.name: location
oaa:properties.description: City, port name, or facility address

oaa:properties.name: typicalDwellTime
oaa:properties.description: Average time cargo spends at this node (e.g. 2-3 days)

oaa:properties.name: maxDwellTime
oaa:properties.description: Maximum acceptable dwell before shelf-life/compliance risk

oaa:properties.name: nodeRiskScore
oaa:properties.description: Inherent risk score 1.0-10.0 for this node

oaa:properties.name: nodeSequence
oaa:properties.description: Order position in the chain (1 = origin)

oaa:properties.name: isCriticalPath
oaa:properties.description: True if delay here delays the entire chain

entities.name: ShipmentLeg
entities.description: A movement segment between two chain nodes. Captures the mode of transport, carrier, duration, temperature requirements, and shipping-specific details (vessel, container, route).

oaa:properties.name: legSequence
oaa:properties.description: Order in the chain (1 = first leg)

oaa:properties.name: distance
oaa:properties.description: Distance in km or nautical miles

oaa:properties.name: route
oaa:properties.description: Route description (e.g. via Suez Canal, via Cape of Good Hope)

oaa:properties.name: insuranceCoverage
oaa:properties.description: Insurance type (e.g. Marine Cargo All-Risk, ICC-A)

entities.name: Shipment
entities.description: An actual consignment of goods moving through the supply chain. Tracks current position, status, and cumulative events.

oaa:properties.name: shelfLifeRemaining
oaa:properties.description: Remaining shelf life at current point (e.g. 45 days)

entities.name: ComplianceGate
entities.description: A regulatory or procedural checkpoint that cargo must pass. Failure to clear a gate blocks forward movement and triggers escalation. Each gate has a pass rate, typical duration, and known failure modes.

oaa:properties.name: authority
oaa:properties.description: Governing body (e.g. DAFF, DEFRA, APHA, HMRC, ABF)

entities.name: TradeDocument
entities.description: A document required in the trade chain — health certificates, customs declarations, bills of lading, certificates of origin, insurance certificates, etc. Documents have lifecycle status and validity periods.

oaa:properties.name: requiredAtGate
oaa:properties.description: Which compliance gate requires this document

oaa:properties.name: leadTimeDays
oaa:properties.description: Days needed to obtain this document

oaa:properties.name: jurisdictionDocType
oaa:properties.description: Jurisdiction-specific document type name (e.g. AQIS_MeatNotice, IPAFFS_Notification, CHED-P) — instance-level override of the generic documentType enum

entities.name: ColdChainEvent
entities.description: A temperature monitoring data point in the cold chain. Continuous logging creates the temperature integrity proof for the shipment. Breaches trigger incidents and escalations.

oaa:properties.name: excursionDuration
oaa:properties.description: How long the temperature has been out of range

entities.name: Party
entities.description: An actor in the logistics chain — exporter, importer, freight forwarder, shipping line, customs broker, government authority, port operator, cold-chain provider, insurer, or bank.

oaa:properties.name: contactSLA
oaa:properties.description: Response time SLA for escalations (e.g. 2 hours)

entities.name: Bottleneck
entities.description: A known tight spot, constraint, or recurring friction point in the chain. Bottlenecks have a type, severity, likelihood, and associated risk/impact assessments. They are attached to specific nodes or legs where they manifest.

oaa:properties.name: typicalDelay
oaa:properties.description: How long this bottleneck typically delays shipments

oaa:properties.name: seasonality
oaa:properties.description: When this bottleneck is most acute

entities.name: RiskAssessment
entities.description: A structured risk assessment attached to a node, leg, bottleneck, or compliance gate. Captures likelihood, impact dimensions, and a composite risk score. Enables heat-map visualisation across the chain.

entities.name: ImpactAssessment
entities.description: Quantifies the net impact of a bottleneck, incident, or risk event across financial, time, reputational, and compliance dimensions. Captures both gross impact and recoveries (insurance, claims) to calculate net impact.

oaa:properties.name: grossFinancialImpact
oaa:properties.description: Total financial loss before recoveries (currency units)

oaa:properties.name: netFinancialImpact
oaa:properties.description: Gross minus all recoveries — the actual bottom-line hit

oaa:properties.name: regulatoryConsequence
oaa:properties.description: Regulatory outcome (e.g. warning letter, licence review, trade suspension)

oaa:properties.name: cascadeEffect
oaa:properties.description: Downstream impacts on other shipments or chains

oaa:properties.name: annualisedExposure
oaa:properties.description: Expected annual loss = netImpact × annual frequency

entities.name: Incident
entities.description: A disruption event that interrupts the normal flow of the supply chain. Incidents are classified by type and severity, occur at a specific node/leg, and trigger escalation paths.

entities.name: Escalation
entities.description: An escalation step in the response pathway. Escalations are tiered (L0-L4), each with a responsible party, time window, and prescribed actions. Escalations chain together — if L1 fails to resolve within its window, it escalates to L2, etc.

oaa:properties.name: triggerCondition
oaa:properties.description: What condition triggers this escalation level

oaa:properties.name: responsibleParty
oaa:properties.description: Role or team responsible at this level

oaa:properties.name: timeToEscalate
oaa:properties.description: Time window before escalating to next level (e.g. 4 hours)

oaa:properties.name: prescribedActions
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated actions to take at this level

oaa:properties.name: decisionAuthority
oaa:properties.description: What decisions can be made at this level

oaa:properties.name: notificationList
oaa:properties.description: Who gets notified at this level

oaa:properties.name: maxFinancialAuthority
oaa:properties.description: Maximum spend authority at this level (e.g. up to AUD 50,000)

entities.name: RiskMitigation
entities.description: A proactive control or contingency measure that reduces the likelihood or impact of a bottleneck or risk. Mitigations have a type (preventive, detective, corrective), cost, and measured effectiveness.

oaa:properties.name: owner
oaa:properties.description: Party responsible for maintaining this control

entities.name: Hypothesis
entities.description: A testable prediction about the supply chain — what we believe will happen under given conditions. Hypotheses have a confidence level, measurement method, and iterate through probabilistic testing. Extends the PE-ONT Hypothesis pattern into logistics context with predictive/historical pattern support.

oaa:properties.name: statement
oaa:properties.description: The testable claim (e.g. 'Chilled beef via Suez will arrive within 28 days 85% of the time')

oaa:properties.name: dataSource
oaa:properties.description: Historical data source for the prediction

oaa:properties.name: learningNote
oaa:properties.description: What was learned from this iteration

oaa:properties.name: nextIteration
oaa:properties.description: Refined hypothesis for next test cycle

entities.name: Scenario
entities.description: A probabilistic what-if scenario that models alternative outcomes for the supply chain. Scenarios have trigger conditions, probability weights, and cascading consequences. Used for planning — 'what happens if the Suez Canal is blocked?' or 'what if UK cold-storage capacity drops 30%?'

oaa:properties.name: timeHorizon
oaa:properties.description: Time window for this scenario (e.g. 'Q3 2026', 'next 12 months')

oaa:properties.name: transitTimeChange
oaa:properties.description: Impact on transit time (e.g. '+12 days', '-2 days')

oaa:properties.name: capacityChange
oaa:properties.description: Impact on available capacity (e.g. '-30% cold storage')

oaa:properties.name: netFinancialImpact
oaa:properties.description: Estimated net financial impact in currency units

oaa:properties.name: mitigationPlan
oaa:properties.description: Pre-planned response if this scenario materialises

oaa:properties.name: predictiveSignals
oaa:properties.description: Early-warning indicators that this scenario is materialising

entities.name: CascadeEffect
entities.description: An unintended or secondary consequence that propagates from a primary disruption. Cascade effects model the domino chain — a port delay causes storage overflow, which causes cost spikes, which causes contract renegotiation, which causes reputational damage. Each effect can trigger further cascades.

oaa:properties.name: triggerDelay
oaa:properties.description: How long after the primary event this cascade manifests (e.g. '3-5 days', 'immediate', '2-4 weeks')

oaa:properties.name: probability
oaa:properties.description: Probability of this cascade given the primary event 0.0-1.0

oaa:properties.name: isIntended
oaa:properties.description: False for unintended consequences, true for expected downstream effects

oaa:properties.name: affectedParties
oaa:properties.description: Who bears the impact of this cascade

oaa:properties.name: financialImpact
oaa:properties.description: Additional financial impact from this cascade

entities.name: PredictivePattern
entities.description: A recurring pattern identified from historical supply chain data. Predictive patterns enable proactive planning — they describe what typically happens under given conditions and provide leading indicators for early warning.

oaa:properties.name: observedFrequency
oaa:properties.description: How often this pattern recurs (e.g. 'annually Q4', 'every 3-5 years', 'monthly')

oaa:properties.name: historicalOccurrences
oaa:properties.description: Number of times observed in data

oaa:properties.name: leadIndicators
oaa:properties.description: Early-warning signals that this pattern is activating

oaa:properties.name: leadTimedays
oaa:properties.description: How many days before the pattern manifests the indicators appear

oaa:properties.name: typicalImpact
oaa:properties.description: What typically happens when this pattern activates

oaa:properties.name: confidenceTrend
oaa:properties.description: Is this pattern becoming more or less reliable?

oaa:properties.name: recommendedAction
oaa:properties.description: Pre-emptive action when pattern is detected

relationships.name: Has Leg
relationships.description: Supply chain contains this shipment leg

relationships.name: Has Node
relationships.description: Supply chain contains this chain node

relationships.name: Connects From
relationships.description: Leg originates at this node

relationships.name: Connects To
relationships.description: Leg terminates at this node

relationships.name: Next Leg
relationships.description: Sequential link to the next leg in the chain

relationships.name: Has Compliance Gate
relationships.description: This node has a regulatory/procedural gate that must be cleared

relationships.name: Requires Document
relationships.description: This compliance gate requires this document to pass

relationships.name: Issued By
relationships.description: Document issued by this party

relationships.name: Has Bottleneck
relationships.description: This node or leg has a known bottleneck/tight spot

relationships.name: Has Risk Assessment
relationships.description: Risk assessment attached to a node, leg, bottleneck, or gate

relationships.name: Has Impact Profile
relationships.description: Bottleneck or risk has a quantified impact profile showing net financial/time/reputational impact

relationships.name: Has Impact
relationships.description: Incident has a measured impact assessment

relationships.name: Triggers Escalation
relationships.description: Incident triggers this escalation pathway

relationships.name: Escalates To
relationships.description: This escalation level chains to the next level if unresolved within time window

relationships.name: Operated By
relationships.description: Node is operated/managed by this party

relationships.name: Carried By
relationships.description: Shipment leg carried by this party (carrier/shipping line/haulier)

relationships.name: Currently At
relationships.description: Shipment is currently located at this node

relationships.name: On Leg
relationships.description: Shipment is currently on this transport leg

relationships.name: Has Cold Chain Event
relationships.description: Shipment has this cold chain monitoring event

relationships.name: Recorded At
relationships.description: Cold chain event was recorded at this node or during this leg

relationships.name: Affects Shipment
relationships.description: Incident affects this shipment

relationships.name: Occurs At Node
relationships.description: Incident occurs at this chain node

relationships.name: Occurs On Leg
relationships.description: Incident occurs during this transport leg

relationships.name: mitigates
relationships.description: Risk mitigation addresses this bottleneck

relationships.name: Owned By Party
relationships.description: Risk or mitigation is owned by this party

relationships.name: Travels Chain
relationships.description: Shipment travels this supply chain route

relationships.name: Maps To Process
relationships.description: Supply chain maps to a PE-ONT process definition

relationships.name: Maps To GRC Requirement
relationships.description: Compliance gate maps to a GRC framework requirement

relationships.name: Party Maps To Org
relationships.description: Party maps to an ORG-ONT organisation

relationships.name: partyMapsToRole
relationships.description: Party maps to a RRR-ONT functional role — bridges logistics actor responsibilities to the risk/requirement/result role taxonomy

relationships.name: Has Hypothesis
relationships.description: Supply chain or leg has a testable hypothesis attached

relationships.name: Has Scenario
relationships.description: Supply chain has a modelled what-if scenario

relationships.name: Scenario Triggers Bottleneck
relationships.description: This scenario would activate or worsen this bottleneck

relationships.name: Scenario Has Impact
relationships.description: Scenario has a quantified impact assessment

relationships.name: Has Cascade
relationships.description: Bottleneck, incident, or scenario triggers this cascade effect

relationships.name: Cascade Leads To
relationships.description: This cascade effect triggers a further cascade (domino chain)

relationships.name: Has Predictive Pattern
relationships.description: Supply chain, node, or leg has an identified predictive pattern

relationships.name: Pattern Validates Hypothesis
relationships.description: Historical pattern provides evidence for or against a hypothesis

relationships.name: Hypothesis Informs Scenario
relationships.description: Hypothesis testing results inform this scenario's probability/parameters

relationships.name: Party Has Org Context
relationships.description: Party maps to an ORG-CONTEXT organisation context — provides industry classification, maturity assessment, product/brand portfolio, and competitive landscape for this logistics actor

relationships.name: Operates In Market
relationships.description: Supply chain operates within this market context — defines the geographic scope, industry verticals, and localisation requirements for the corridor

relationships.name: Node In Jurisdiction
relationships.description: Chain node sits within this market segment's jurisdiction — enables roll-up of node-level regulatory context to Foundation market geography

businessRules.name: OriginAndDestinationRequired
businessRules.condition: IF a SupplyChain is defined
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one ChainNode with nodeType=Origin AND at least one with nodeType=FinalDestination

businessRules.name: ColdChainContinuity
businessRules.condition: IF commodityClass IN [Frozen, Chilled]
businessRules.action: THEN every ChainNode in the chain MUST have temperatureCapability matching the commodityClass, AND every ShipmentLeg MUST have temperatureSetpoint defined

businessRules.name: FrozenTemperatureThreshold
businessRules.condition: IF commodityClass = Frozen AND a ColdChainEvent.temperature > -18.0
businessRules.action: THEN inRange MUST be false AND an Incident of type TemperatureBreach MUST be created

businessRules.name: ChilledTemperatureThreshold
businessRules.condition: IF commodityClass = Chilled AND a ColdChainEvent.temperature NOT BETWEEN 0.0 AND 5.0
businessRules.action: THEN inRange MUST be false AND an Incident of type TemperatureBreach MUST be created

businessRules.name: EscalationLevelProgression
businessRules.condition: IF an Escalation.escalatesTo references another Escalation
businessRules.action: THEN the target escalation level MUST be higher than the source (L0 < L1 < L2 < L3 < L4)

businessRules.name: ComplianceGateDocumentation
businessRules.condition: IF a ComplianceGate has isMandatory = true
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have at least one requiresDocument relationship

businessRules.name: LegSequenceConnectivity
businessRules.condition: IF ShipmentLeg[n].nextLeg references ShipmentLeg[n+1]
businessRules.action: THEN ShipmentLeg[n].connectsTo MUST equal ShipmentLeg[n+1].connectsFrom (no gaps in the chain)

businessRules.name: IncidentRequiresEscalation
businessRules.condition: IF an Incident has severity IN [Critical, Major]
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a triggersEscalation relationship with escalationLevel >= L2_Management

businessRules.name: NetImpactCalculation
businessRules.condition: IF an ImpactAssessment exists
businessRules.action: THEN netFinancialImpact MUST equal grossFinancialImpact minus (insuranceRecovery + otherRecoveries)

businessRules.name: ShelfLifeMonitoring
businessRules.condition: IF a Shipment.shelfLifeRemaining < 14 days AND status NOT IN [Delivered, WrittenOff]
businessRules.action: THEN an Incident of severity >= Moderate SHOULD be raised with escalationLevel >= L1_Operational

businessRules.name: CriticalPathDelay
businessRules.condition: IF a ChainNode.isCriticalPath = true AND actual dwell time exceeds maxDwellTime
businessRules.action: THEN an Incident MUST be raised AND escalation MUST be triggered

businessRules.name: RiskScoreComposite
businessRules.condition: IF a RiskAssessment is created
businessRules.action: THEN compositeRiskScore MUST equal likelihood × MAX(financialImpact, timeImpact, reputationalImpact, complianceImpact)

businessRules.name: DocumentValidityAtGate
businessRules.condition: IF a Shipment arrives at a ComplianceGate
businessRules.action: THEN all required TradeDocuments MUST have status = Issued or Verified AND validTo >= current date

businessRules.name: InspectionFailureConsequence
businessRules.condition: IF a ComplianceGate of gateType = SanitaryPhytosanitary has failureConsequence = Rejection
businessRules.action: THEN the Incident MUST trigger escalation at minimum L3_Executive AND regulatory notification is required

businessRules.name: HypothesisRequiresMeasurement
businessRules.condition: IF a Hypothesis has status = Active
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have a measurementMethod defined AND confidenceLevel MUST be between 0.0 and 1.0

businessRules.name: ScenarioProbabilityBound
businessRules.condition: IF a Scenario exists
businessRules.action: THEN probability MUST be between 0.0 and 1.0 AND the sum of all scenario probabilities for a SupplyChain SHOULD NOT exceed 1.0 for mutually exclusive scenarios

businessRules.name: CascadeChainDepth
businessRules.condition: IF a CascadeEffect.cascadeLeadsTo chain exceeds depth 5
businessRules.action: THEN the deepest cascade SHOULD be flagged for review — excessive cascade depth may indicate modelling error or systemic fragility requiring structural mitigation

businessRules.name: InvalidatedHypothesisIteration
businessRules.condition: IF a Hypothesis.status = Invalidated
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have actualOutcome, variance, and learningNote populated AND nextIteration SHOULD reference a refined hypothesis

businessRules.name: PredictivePatternReliability
businessRules.condition: IF a PredictivePattern.reliability < 0.5
businessRules.action: THEN confidenceTrend SHOULD be 'Weakening' AND the pattern SHOULD NOT be used for automated decision-making without human review

businessRules.name: UnintendedCascadeMustBeTracked
businessRules.condition: IF a CascadeEffect.isIntended = false AND severity IN [Critical, Major]
businessRules.action: THEN the cascade MUST be linked to a RiskMitigation or an Escalation for tracking

changeControl.author: PF-Core
changes: Initial release — 18 entities, 38 relationships, 20 business rules, 7 join patterns
Designed for AU→UK meat trade corridor as reference implementation
Risk scoring with 5-dimension impact model (financial, time, reputational, compliance, composite)
Net impact assessment with insurance/recovery offsets
5-level escalation model (L0 auto → L4 external/governmental)
Cold chain integrity tracking with automated breach detection
Cross-ontology bridges to PE-ONT, ORG-ONT, GRC-FW-ONT
Hypothesis testing with iterative measurement, confidence intervals, and variance tracking
Scenario modelling with probability-weighted impact projections
Cascade/domino effect chains for unintended consequence mapping
Predictive pattern recognition with lead indicators and reliability scoring

changeControl.author: PF-Core
changes: NEUTRALITY FIX — removed corridor-specific document types (AQIS_MeatNotice, IPAFFS_Notification, CHED_P) from TradeDocument enum; replaced with generic types (RegulatoryNotification, PreNotification, EntryDocument, TransitDocument, SanitaryCertificate). Added jurisdictionDocType property for instance-level override.

FOUNDATION ROLL-UP — added ORG-CONTEXT-ONT import (v1.0.1) with 3 new bridge relationships: partyHasOrgContext → OrganizationContext, operatesInMarket → MarketContext, nodeInJurisdiction → MarketSegment
Added 2 Foundation roll-up join patterns: JP-LSC-008 (Party Context & Maturity Roll-Up), JP-LSC-009 (Corridor Market & Jurisdiction Roll-Up)
Totals now: 18 entities, 41 relationships, 20 business rules, 9 join patterns. Imports: PE-ONT, ORG-ONT, RRR-ONT, GRC-FW-ONT, ORG-CONTEXT-ONT.

changeControl.author: PF-Core
changes: OAA v7.0.0 COMPLIANCE UPGRADE — full pass on gates G1-G4, G7, G20-G24
G20 fix: Replaced 18 generic placeholder competency questions with domain-specific questions referencing target relationships and business rules
G8 fix (advisory): Renamed relationship 'Mitigates' → 'mitigates' for camelCase compliance (41/41 now pass)
Added partyMapsToRole relationship (lsc:Party → pf:FunctionalRole) — resolves orphaned RRR-ONT import, completes 6th cross-ontology bridge
Totals now: 18 entities, 42 relationships, 20 business rules, 9 join patterns, 18 competency questions. 6 cross-ontology bridges (PE, ORG, RRR, GRC-FW, ORG-CONTEXT ×3).

competencyQuestions.question: What trade routes and commodity types does this supply chain corridor serve, and what is its aggregate risk score?
Which chain nodes are on the critical path, and what are their dwell time constraints and temperature capabilities?
What is the ordered sequence of transport legs from origin to destination, and which legs carry the highest risk?
What is the current status and location of a shipment, and how much shelf life remains?
Which regulatory gates must cargo clear at each node, what are their historical pass rates, and what happens on failure?
Which documents are required at each compliance gate, who issues them, and what is their current validity status?
Has the cold chain been maintained within tolerance throughout the journey, and where did temperature excursions occur?
Which parties are responsible for each segment of the supply chain, and what are their SLA commitments and risk ownership?
Where are the known bottlenecks in the chain, what is their severity and likelihood, and what mitigation strategies exist?
What is the composite risk score across all dimensions for a given node, leg, or bottleneck, and what is the residual risk after controls?
What is the net financial impact of a disruption after insurance and other recoveries are applied, and what is the annualised exposure?
What incidents have occurred on this supply chain, what was the root cause, and what was the time-to-detect and time-to-resolve?
What is the escalation pathway for a critical incident, which parties hold decision authority at each level, and what is the time window?
What preventive and corrective controls are in place to mitigate each bottleneck, and what is their measured effectiveness and cost?
What testable predictions have been made about this supply chain, what is their confidence level, and how have they been validated or invalidated?
What probabilistic what-if scenarios have been modelled for this corridor, what are their trigger conditions and net financial impact?
What downstream cascade effects could a primary disruption trigger, which are reversible, and what is the domino chain depth?
What recurring patterns have been identified from historical data, what are their leading indicators and reliability scores?
